# [PUB-03] Post-silicon measurement paper

> Plan ID `PUB-03` — local plan identifier in this issue pack, **not** a
> GitHub issue number. See [`00_EPIC_2026.md`](00_EPIC_2026.md).

## Status

❌ **Gated on SILICON.** [`STATUS.md`](../../STATUS.md) shows SILICON
at ❌ ("not yet"). No measured power, energy, TOPS-per-watt, or
demo-workload latency is claimed by this repo.

## Plan (post-silicon only)

1. Create `docs/silicon/` with one sub-file per demo workload from
   `BENCHMARKS.md` (Demo 1 / Demo 2 / Demo 3).
2. Fill each sub-file with the **measured** counterpart of every
   estimate in the corresponding `BENCHMARKS.md` table. Do **not**
   pre-fill.
3. Flip the SILICON row in `STATUS.md` from ❌ to ✅ only after a
   third party can reproduce the measurements from the clean clone.
4. Draft and submit the measurement paper citing the
   `docs/silicon/` files.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Silicon returned and packaged (line-wide, not γ-only).
- [ ] `docs/silicon/` populated with measured tables.
- [ ] Measurement paper draft + submission record.
- [ ] `BENCHMARKS.md` cross-links the measured-table files.

## R5 honesty

- **No silicon return date is asserted.** The fabrication timeline
  is owned by the shuttle and is outside this repo.
- The README headline `75 / 405` TOPS/W remain SKY130A pre-silicon
  estimates and **stay labelled as such until** this issue closes
  with a real `docs/silicon/` table.
- Closing this issue requires a third party to reproduce the
  measured tables from the clean clone, not just an internal report.
